title: cichorium intybus subsp. foliosum
intro: Cichorium intybus subsp. foliosum is a subspecies of the common chicory plant, characterized by its leafy foliage and dandelion-like appearance. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and support liver function due to its high levels of inulin and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Cichorium intybus subsp. foliosum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or added to capsule formulations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
